sections.title: Constitutional Basis
sections.content: Article II, Section 16 of the 1987 Philippine Constitution recognizes the people's right to a healthy and harmonious environment. The Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR) is the lead agency for environmental management and protection, with six specialized bureaus: Forest Management Bureau, Lands Management Bureau, Mines and Geo-Sciences Bureau, Environmental Management Bureau (EMB), Ecosystems Research and Development Bureau, and Protected Areas and Wildlife Bureau.

sections.title: Key Environmental Laws
rows: R.A. 8749 — Philippine Clean Air Act of 1999

Strives to attain clean air quality aligned with National Air Quality guidelines while minimizing adverse economic effects. LGUs have primary responsibility; area-based responsibility; clean air is a shared concern.
P.D. 1181 — Pollution Control Law
Ensures reduction, prevention, and control of vehicular pollution by defining maximum allowable emissions.
R.A. 9275 — Philippine Clean Water Act of 2004
Safeguards water resources from pollution from land-based sources.
R.A. 9003 — Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000
Implements organized solid waste management; mandatory MRFs in barangays; prohibition of open dumps; mandatory waste segregation at source.
R.A. 6969 — Toxic Substances, Hazardous and Nuclear Waste Control Act of 1990
DENR authority to control importation, production, processing, sale, distribution, use, and disposal of chemical substances; forbids introduction of hazardous and nuclear waste.
P.D. 1586 — Environmental Impact Statement (EIS) System of 1978
Created the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) System; ECC required for Environmentally Critical Projects.
R.A. 9729 — Climate Change Act of 2009
Incorporates climate change into government policy; creates the Climate Change Commission.
R.A. 9512 — Environmental Awareness and Education Act of 2008
Promotes environmental awareness through education.

sections.title: Characteristics of Hazardous Waste
items: Toxicity — poisonous; carcinogenic, teratogenic, or mutagenic effects
Ignitability — can create fire under certain conditions
Corrosivity — acidic or basic; capable of corroding metals
Reactivity — unstable; can react violently with water; may detonate or produce toxic gases
Examples: chemical waste, toxic waste, medical waste, industrial waste, radioactive waste

sections.title: Pollution Control Officer (PCO)
items: A person with technical expertise in pollution control; officially accredited by the EMB Regional Office.
Required for: establishments releasing solid, liquid, or gaseous waste; LGUs, Development Authorities, GOCCs, and other public establishments.
NOT required for: ambulant vendors and stall owners under R.A. 6977; Material Recovery Facilities operated by barangays (those not handling hazardous waste).
Duties: ensure legal compliance; handle all permits and registrations for pollution sources; actively participate in technical conferences; initiate environmental management activities.

sections.title: Environmental Impact Statement (EIS) System
items: ECC (Environmental Compliance Certificate) — issued by DENR/EMB; certifies compliance with EIS requirements.
No individual, partnership, or corporation may undertake an Environmentally Critical Project (ECP) or operate in an Environmentally Critical Area (ECA) without an ECC.
ECC Validity: Valid for the entire project lifespan unless there is significant expansion, change in technology/location, or the project is not implemented within 5 years from issuance.
Certificate of Non-Coverage — document stating a project falls outside the EIS system scope (does not exempt from other environmental laws).

sections.title: Priority Programs of DENR
items: 1. Geo-Hazard, Groundwater Assessment and Responsible Mining: Rehabilitation of abandoned mines; assessment of coastal geo-hazards.
2. Enhanced National Greening Program: Rehabilitation of 1.2 million hectares of denuded forest lands. Goal: Plant 1.5 billion trees in 1.5 million hectares by 2028.
3. Intensified Environmental Protection — Clean Air Program: Motor Vehicle Emission Management; Industrial Emission Management; Roadside and General Ambient Air Monitoring.
4. Intensified Forest Protection and Anti-Illegal Logging: Neutralizing illegal logging hotspots; forest fire prevention; continuous forest patrolling through the Lawin System.

sections.title: Chemical Management Tools
rows: Philippine Inventory of Chemicals & Chemical Substances
Comprehensive list of all chemicals currently in use or traded in the Philippines
Priority Chemical List
Compiled by DENR; lists chemicals posing unreasonable risks to public health, workplace safety, and the environment
Chemical Control Order (CCO)
Regulatory instrument to restrict/limit/regulate handling, use, manufacture, import, export, transport, storage, and distribution of chemicals on the Priority Chemical List
